1: Is Is of few days, and full of trouble.
2: He fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.
3: And bringest me into judgment with thee?
5: Thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;
6: Till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.
7: And that the tender branch thereof will not cease.
8: And the stock thereof die in the ground;
9: And bring forth boughs like a plant.
10: Yea, man giveth up the ghost, and where is is he?
11: And the flood decayeth and drieth up:
12: Nor be raised out of their sleep.
13: That thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!
15: Thou wilt have a desire to the work of thine hands.
16: Dost thou not watch over my sin?
17: And thou sewest up mine iniquity.
18: And the rock is removed out of his place.
19: And thou destroyest the hope of man.
20: Thou changest his countenance, and sendest him away.
21: And they are brought low, but he perceiveth it it not of them.
22: And his soul within him shall mourn.
